payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: prd_review.

Intent:
Persist per-run quality trend metrics and expose a read-only per-task_class/per-gate trend query.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: P1 persisted per-run/per-gate metrics derived from ledger (supervisor_final_status checked before agent-influenced status)
P2 bounded sampled false-accept audit reuses collect_runtime_evidence runtime floor
P3 read-only query+CLI grouped by task_class/gate performs no writes
P4 metrics cannot advance/block/override/mutate any gate

criteria: all four PRD promises trace to real named source symbols
observational-only invariant enforced at run-end wiring (success+failure)
evidence: quality_trends.py sampled P11 audit (temp git repo, missing deliverable -> false_accept_count==1)
metrics emit no dual_agent_gate_result / gate_authority unchanged
confidence.rationale: Source symbols backing all four promises grep-confirmed at memory-matching lines; observational-only wiring read directly and verified non-gating on both branches; PRD is well-formed with explicit out-of-scope. Residual: pytest not executed (test_status unknown) and PRD sha not re-confirmed (approval-gated) though content read directly.

payload.content: prd_review ACCEPT: all four PRD promises (P1 persist trend metrics, P2 sampled P11 false-accept audit, P3 read-only query/CLI, P4 observational-only) map to real named source boundaries; observational-only invariant verified at run-end wiring; grill findings 3/3 resolved.

assumptions_to_verify: supervisor_final_status is consulted before claude_gate_status in the accepted-status derivation (verified in prior gate; re-assert at tdd/outcome)
CLI path returns read_only==True and writes zero trend rows
contradictions_checked: whether run-end trend recording can block/fail the gate -> no: try/except, failure path keeps gate_authority unchanged
whether query_quality_trends mutates rows/events -> SELECT-only GROUP BY per source and PRD testing decision
whether metrics emit dual_agent_gate_result -> PRD asserts no, covered by a named test
missing_evidence: pytest execution results for the quality_trends test suite
reconfirmed sha256 of prd.md against handoff (6e74c9...) -- approval-gated, content read directly instead
live Postgres parity of supervisor_quality_trends table (impl decision asserts structural alignment only)
critical_review.strongest_objection: P1 promise 'derived from ledger rather than agent self-report' is partly undermined because the acceptance determination's fallback chain can consult an agent-influenced gate status; a PRD promise should not have an agent-self-report escape hatch.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If P4 observational-only were violated (any predicate reading trend metrics to affect gate authority), or if a promise lacked any backing source symbol, I would move to revise/deny.

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:
Persist per-run quality trend metrics and expose a read-only per-task_class/per-gate trend query.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: issues.md decomposes work into 3 slices covering PRD P1-P4 with no orphans
Every acceptance criterion traces to a source symbol and a test
SQLite and Postgres state interfaces expose identical trend helper method names

criteria: All 4 PRD promises mapped to slices with no orphans
Each AC backed by a real source symbol
Each AC backed by a named test
Observational-only invariant enforced in source and tested
confidence.rationale: All slices, PRD mappings, and ACs verified against current source and tests by direct read; parity confirmed by symbol presence in both lanes. Lowered from higher because pytest was not executed in this gate (self_reported), Postgres parity verified structurally (method names) not by live PG run, and the audit-path non-mutation lacks a dedicated test.

payload.content: Issues decomposition for quality trend metrics maps 3 slices to PRD P1-P4 with no orphans; every AC is source+test backed across SQLite and Postgres lanes. Accept.

assumptions_to_verify: Postgres helpers produce identical column/aggregate shape as SQLite at runtime
Boundary-name vs symbol-name mismatch is intentional descriptive naming, resolved at implementation stage
contradictions_checked: Whether any slice promises a PRD contract not in prd.md — none; P1-P4 all present
Whether query path could mutate — test:240/:275 assert row and event counts unchanged
Whether audit alters gate decisions — source returns gate_authority unchanged and observational_only flags
missing_evidence: pytest pass/fail not executed in this gate
Live-Postgres execution of upsert/update_audit/query (parity verified by symbol presence only)
Dedicated test asserting run_sampled_p11_false_accept_audit writes no dual_agent_gate_result events
critical_review.strongest_objection: Slice 1/2 public boundary names in issues.md do not exactly match the implemented function symbols, and the sampled-audit code path has no dedicated non-mutation test (only record+query non-mutation is tested at :314).
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A slice promising a contract absent from the PRD, an AC with no corresponding test or source symbol, or evidence that the audit/query path mutates gate events or trend rows.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Persist per-run quality trend metrics and expose a read-only per-task_class/per-gate trend query.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: 8 plan tests map P1-P4 with no orphans
All referenced symbols exist in current source
Grill findings F1-F3 resolved by dedicated tests
Tests are non-vacuous (exact value/aggregate assertions, invariant counts)

criteria: tests map to PRD promises P1-P4
confidence.rationale: All 8 tests verified against real symbols and shown non-vacuous; aligns with prior ACCEPT of same plan. Capped below 0.9 because pytest was not executed here (test_status self-reported) and tests are green-not-red against pre-existing implementation.

payload.content: TDD plan defines 8 non-vacuous tests mapping P1-P4 with correct Red/Green; all named symbols exist in current source (record_quality_trends_for_run, run_sampled_p11_false_accept_audit, query_quality_trends; state upsert/update_audit/query/count) and the migration test asserts v8 table+index. Grill F1-F3 resolved. Accept.

assumptions_to_verify: run_forward_migrations actually creates version 8 at runtime
CLI script run_supervisor_trend_metrics.py performs no writes in query mode
contradictions_checked: status-precedence test isolates supervisor_final_status vs claude_gate_status (proven) though not vs status (both blocked)
P11 audit creates a genuine false-accept rather than replaying receipts
missing_evidence: live pytest pass/fail output
git-confirmed RED state before implementation
critical_review.strongest_objection: Tests are GREEN against already-implemented untracked source rather than demonstrated RED-first, and pytest was not run in this review, so test_status is self-reported.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A named plan test referencing a non-existent symbol, a vacuous test that passes regardless of correct behavior, or a P1-P4 promise with no covering test.

payload.content:
Independently review the tdd_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

cursor_review.transcript_tail:
present and untracked; tests are green against existing code, not demonstrated red-first. For `tdd_review`, the *plan artifact* is still sound, but TDD discipline for downstream gates is weakened.

### Secondary gaps (note for implementation gate)

- **P4 fidelity:** Plan Green says “gate/workflow authority untouched”; `test_quality_trends_metrics_do_not_advance_or_block_gates` only checks gate-event IDs, not workflow step status.
- **Status precedence isolation:** `test_quality_trends_prefers_supervisor_final_status_over_claude_status` sets `status=blocked` alongside `supervisor_final_status=blocked`; it does not isolate precedence vs bare `status=accepted`.
- **Postgres parity:** Helpers exist in `postgres_state.py`, but no TDD-plan test covers Postgres lane (deferred to implementation plan).

### What would change the decision

A plan test referencing a missing symbol, a vacuous assertion, a P1–P4 promise without a covering test, or pytest failures on the named tests.

cursor_review.transcript_tail:
present and untracked; tests are green against existing code, not demonstrated red-first. For `tdd_review`, the *plan artifact* is still sound, but TDD discipline for downstream gates is weakened.

### Secondary gaps (note for implementation gate)

- **P4 fidelity:** Plan Green says “gate/workflow authority untouched”; `test_quality_trends_metrics_do_not_advance_or_block_gates` only checks gate-event IDs, not workflow step status.
- **Status precedence isolation:** `test_quality_trends_prefers_supervisor_final_status_over_claude_status` sets `status=blocked` alongside `supervisor_final_status=blocked`; it does not isolate precedence vs bare `status=accepted`.
- **Postgres parity:** Helpers exist in `postgres_state.py`, but no TDD-plan test covers Postgres lane (deferred to implementation plan).

### What would change the decision

A plan test referencing a missing symbol, a vacuous assertion, a P1–P4 promise without a covering test, or pytest failures on the named tests.

independent_reviewer.transcript_tail:
present and untracked; tests are green against existing code, not demonstrated red-first. For `tdd_review`, the *plan artifact* is still sound, but TDD discipline for downstream gates is weakened.

### Secondary gaps (note for implementation gate)

- **P4 fidelity:** Plan Green says “gate/workflow authority untouched”; `test_quality_trends_metrics_do_not_advance_or_block_gates` only checks gate-event IDs, not workflow step status.
- **Status precedence isolation:** `test_quality_trends_prefers_supervisor_final_status_over_claude_status` sets `status=blocked` alongside `supervisor_final_status=blocked`; it does not isolate precedence vs bare `status=accepted`.
- **Postgres parity:** Helpers exist in `postgres_state.py`, but no TDD-plan test covers Postgres lane (deferred to implementation plan).

### What would change the decision

A plan test referencing a missing symbol, a vacuous assertion, a P1–P4 promise without a covering test, or pytest failures on the named tests.

independent_reviewer.transcript_tail:
present and untracked; tests are green against existing code, not demonstrated red-first. For `tdd_review`, the *plan artifact* is still sound, but TDD discipline for downstream gates is weakened.

### Secondary gaps (note for implementation gate)

- **P4 fidelity:** Plan Green says “gate/workflow authority untouched”; `test_quality_trends_metrics_do_not_advance_or_block_gates` only checks gate-event IDs, not workflow step status.
- **Status precedence isolation:** `test_quality_trends_prefers_supervisor_final_status_over_claude_status` sets `status=blocked` alongside `supervisor_final_status=blocked`; it does not isolate precedence vs bare `status=accepted`.
- **Postgres parity:** Helpers exist in `postgres_state.py`, but no TDD-plan test covers Postgres lane (deferred to implementation plan).

### What would change the decision

A plan test referencing a missing symbol, a vacuous assertion, a P1–P4 promise without a covering test, or pytest failures on the named tests.

assumptions_to_verify: Expected semantics are that time_to_accepted_outcome_s is null or excluded when the final gate status is blocked.
Expected revision_rounds should not ignore a final reviewer/runtime block after an earlier accepted event.
Focused tests pass in a writable environment with cache/temp writes available.
CLI query initialization or schema migration side effects are acceptable for the read-only query contract.
contradictions_checked: Claude/old objection that P4 only checks gate-result ids was checked against current tests and is contradicted by current workflow/step immutability assertions.
TDD artifact sha256 matched the supplied receipt.
All 8 plan-named tests exist verbatim.
supervisor_final_status is consulted before status and claude_gate_status in source, but the test does not isolate supervisor_final_status versus status.
SQLite and Postgres state methods for upsert, audit update, query, and count are present.
Live Postgres behavior was not verified.
missing_evidence: Focused pytest output for tests/test_quality_trends.py and the migration tests.
Captured RED-before-implementation run for the 8 named tests.
A test for an initially accepted gate that is finally blocked, asserting accepted=false and no stale accepted-outcome metrics.
A complete changed-files receipt that includes schema, MCP run-end wiring, and Postgres parity test changes.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest reason not to advance is that final gate authority is not fully proven for metrics: tests cover accepted -> blocked -> accepted, but not accepted -> blocked final. In source, record_quality_trends_for_run selects the last accepted event before checking whether it is final, so a finally blocked gate can still carry stale time_to_accepted_outcome_s and revision_rounds from an earlier accepted event.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Add or point to a public-boundary test for accepted -> final blocked metrics, fix the stale accepted-event behavior if confirmed, provide focused pytest output, and update the changed-files receipt to cover all relevant trend wiring.

critical_review.strongest_objection: Strict TDD process was violated: implementation landed before tdd_review first-round clearance and no captured RED transition exists; only retrospective GREEN evidence is available.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Any of the 8 tests failing under pytest, a missing or renamed plan test, a PRD promise (P1-P4) with no mapped test, or discovery that tdd.md hash diverges from handoff.

assumptions_to_verify: Expected semantics are that time_to_accepted_outcome_s is null or excluded when the final gate status is blocked.
Expected revision_rounds should not ignore a final reviewer/runtime block after an earlier accepted event.
Focused tests pass in a writable environment with cache/temp writes available.
CLI query initialization or schema migration side effects are acceptable for the read-only query contract.
contradictions_checked: Claude/old objection that P4 only checks gate-result ids was checked against current tests and is contradicted by current workflow/step immutability assertions.
TDD artifact sha256 matched the supplied receipt.
All 8 plan-named tests exist verbatim.
supervisor_final_status is consulted before status and claude_gate_status in source, but the test does not isolate supervisor_final_status versus status.
SQLite and Postgres state methods for upsert, audit update, query, and count are present.
Live Postgres behavior was not verified.
missing_evidence: Focused pytest output for tests/test_quality_trends.py and the migration tests.
Captured RED-before-implementation run for the 8 named tests.
A test for an initially accepted gate that is finally blocked, asserting accepted=false and no stale accepted-outcome metrics.
A complete changed-files receipt that includes schema, MCP run-end wiring, and Postgres parity test changes.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest reason not to advance is that final gate authority is not fully proven for metrics: tests cover accepted -> blocked -> accepted, but not accepted -> blocked final. In source, record_quality_trends_for_run selects the last accepted event before checking whether it is final, so a finally blocked gate can still carry stale time_to_accepted_outcome_s and revision_rounds from an earlier accepted event.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Add or point to a public-boundary test for accepted -> final blocked metrics, fix the stale accepted-event behavior if confirmed, provide focused pytest output, and update the changed-files receipt to cover all relevant trend wiring.

strongest_objection.text: The strongest reason not to advance is that final gate authority is not fully proven for metrics: tests cover accepted -> blocked -> accepted, but not accepted -> blocked final. In source, record_quality_trends_for_run selects the last accepted event before checking whether it is final, so a finally blocked gate can still carry stale time_to_accepted_outcome_s and revision_rounds from an earlier accepted event.

assumptions_to_verify: Expected semantics are that time_to_accepted_outcome_s is null or excluded when the final gate status is blocked.
Expected revision_rounds should not ignore a final reviewer/runtime block after an earlier accepted event.
Focused tests pass in a writable environment with cache/temp writes available.
CLI query initialization or schema migration side effects are acceptable for the read-only query contract.
contradictions_checked: Claude/old objection that P4 only checks gate-result ids was checked against current tests and is contradicted by current workflow/step immutability assertions.
TDD artifact sha256 matched the supplied receipt.
All 8 plan-named tests exist verbatim.
supervisor_final_status is consulted before status and claude_gate_status in source, but the test does not isolate supervisor_final_status versus status.
SQLite and Postgres state methods for upsert, audit update, query, and count are present.
Live Postgres behavior was not verified.
missing_evidence: Focused pytest output for tests/test_quality_trends.py and the migration tests.
Captured RED-before-implementation run for the 8 named tests.
A test for an initially accepted gate that is finally blocked, asserting accepted=false and no stale accepted-outcome metrics.
A complete changed-files receipt that includes schema, MCP run-end wiring, and Postgres parity test changes.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest reason not to advance is that final gate authority is not fully proven for metrics: tests cover accepted -> blocked -> accepted, but not accepted -> blocked final. In source, record_quality_trends_for_run selects the last accepted event before checking whether it is final, so a finally blocked gate can still carry stale time_to_accepted_outcome_s and revision_rounds from an earlier accepted event.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Add or point to a public-boundary test for accepted -> final blocked metrics, fix the stale accepted-event behavior if confirmed, provide focused pytest output, and update the changed-files receipt to cover all relevant trend wiring.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Persist per-run quality trend metrics and expose a read-only per-task_class/per-gate trend query.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
independent_reviewer_non_accept: independent-reviewer-1

claims: 9 plan tests present in source with no orphans.
Corrective test #3 (stale-block) is new and non-vacuous; impl enforces final-event acceptance guard.
P11 audit test asserts false_accept_count==1, denominator==1, rate==1.0 (non-vacuous via missing deliverable in real git repo).
Migration test asserts version 8 table with 14 columns and task_gate index.
Observational-only invariant test asserts no gate-result events or workflow-step mutation.

criteria: All 9 named plan tests exist in source
Corrective stale-acceptance test present and non-vacuous
confidence.rationale: Plan-to-source fidelity, symbol existence, and the corrective test semantics are all directly verified against current files; the implementation logic genuinely depends on each asserted behavior. Confidence is held below 0.9 because pytest was not executed in this gate (test_status self-reported unknown) and the tests are observed GREEN rather than demonstrably RED-first.

Migration v8 test asserts 14-column table plus task_gate index. Residual: GREEN-not-RED and pytest un-run, so test_status is self-reported unknown.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Persist per-run quality trend metrics and expose a read-only per-task_class/per-gate trend query.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
independent_reviewer_blocking_objection: independent-reviewer-1

claims: 10 declared files map exactly to git changes with no orphans
Every PRD promise P1-P4 has at least one mapped, named, existing test
Run-end trend recording is best-effort telemetry that does not advance or block gates
Postgres and SQLite schema lanes are structurally equivalent and migration-backed
Prior reviewer-1 changed_files omission is corrected in this outcome

criteria: File ownership EXACT vs git: confirmed
All 7 traceability tests exist verbatim: confirmed
Steps map to real source symbols: confirmed
Observational-only / gate_authority unchanged: confirmed in source
Live Postgres parity executed: NOT confirmed (structural only)
confidence.rationale: All file ownership, traceability tests, implementation symbols, observational-only wiring, and Postgres parity are confirmed against current source by direct grep/diff. Confidence held below 0.95 because pytest was not executed (test_status unknown) and Postgres parity is structural rather than live-PG validated.

payload.content: Implementation_plan gate accepted. Plan decomposes a read-only quality-trend subsystem across 10 declared files; git shows exactly those 10 changes (6 modified +566/-40, 4 untracked new) with no orphans. All 7 traceability tests exist verbatim in current source; Steps 1-6 map to real symbols; run-end wiring is best-effort observational_only with gate_authority unchanged; Postgres/SQLite parity plus Alembic migration verified. Prior independent-reviewer-1 blocking gap (changed_files omitting codex_supervisor_stdio.py and test_postgres_ledger_lane.py) is resolved here by listing all 10 files.

assumptions_to_verify: The current dirty/untracked worktree is the intended review target
Read-only query means no event/trend-row mutation, not no schema initialization on State construction
P11 sampled-audit semantics permit sampling accepted evidence events
contradictions_checked: Plan's 10 declared files vs git changes: exact match, no orphans
Reviewer-1 prior note that codex_supervisor_stdio.py and test_postgres_ledger_lane.py were omitted from changed_files: both now included
Observational-only claim vs source: run-end recording wraps record_quality_trends_for_run as best-effort with gate_authority unchanged, not a gate advance/block path
All 7 named traceability tests claimed vs present in source: all found verbatim

assumptions_to_verify: Runtime-native receipts are trustworthy and correspond to the reviewed worktree snapshot.
Production deployments run the Alembic migration chain in the same order as the inline schema.
lesson_task_class is populated on normal workflow routes so trend grouping does not collapse to general.
contradictions_checked: Claude said tests were not run in its review env, but supervisor runtime receipts for outcome_review report passed tests; I treat Claude test_status unknown as reviewer-local, not gate-level.
Prompt tdd.md receipt hash mismatched current file; current handoff, outcome-review, skill-receipts, and local shasum agree, indicating stale packet evidence.
Observed run-end wiring records quality_trends in a try/except and returns gate_authority unchanged, with no call path that advances or blocks gates from metrics.
Status precedence in _payload_accepted checks supervisor_final_status before status and claude_gate_status, and the regression test covers Claude accepted versus supervisor blocked.
Query methods are SELECT-only and tests assert event/row count invariance for query and CLI query.
missing_evidence: Live Postgres migration run with CODEX_SUPERVISOR_POSTGRES_TEST_DSN set.
Live Postgres upsert/query/audit helper exercise for supervisor_quality_trends, not just schema shape.
Fresh local pytest rerun by this reviewer; current decision relies on runtime-native isolated-worktree receipts.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest reason not to advance is that Postgres production behavior is only structurally supported here: table DDL, Alembic migration text, and inline-schema equivalence are present, but live Postgres migration/helper execution was not demonstrated in the inspected evidence.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing focused pytest run, a live Postgres migration/helper failure, evidence that quality trend recording can alter gate/workflow authority, or a current artifact hash mismatch in the handoff/outcome-review records would change this to revise.

objections: Claude outcome lists tests/test_postgres_ledger_lane.py::supervisor_quality_trends_parity which pytest cannot collect; actual parity is structural tests in same file

assumptions_to_verify: Runtime-native receipts are trustworthy and correspond to the reviewed worktree snapshot.
Production deployments run the Alembic migration chain in the same order as the inline schema.
lesson_task_class is populated on normal workflow routes so trend grouping does not collapse to general.
contradictions_checked: Claude said tests were not run in its review env, but supervisor runtime receipts for outcome_review report passed tests; I treat Claude test_status unknown as reviewer-local, not gate-level.
Prompt tdd.md receipt hash mismatched current file; current handoff, outcome-review, skill-receipts, and local shasum agree, indicating stale packet evidence.
Observed run-end wiring records quality_trends in a try/except and returns gate_authority unchanged, with no call path that advances or blocks gates from metrics.
Status precedence in _payload_accepted checks supervisor_final_status before status and claude_gate_status, and the regression test covers Claude accepted versus supervisor blocked.
Query methods are SELECT-only and tests assert event/row count invariance for query and CLI query.
missing_evidence: Live Postgres migration run with CODEX_SUPERVISOR_POSTGRES_TEST_DSN set.
Live Postgres upsert/query/audit helper exercise for supervisor_quality_trends, not just schema shape.
Fresh local pytest rerun by this reviewer; current decision relies on runtime-native isolated-worktree receipts.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest reason not to advance is that Postgres production behavior is only structurally supported here: table DDL, Alembic migration text, and inline-schema equivalence are present, but live Postgres migration/helper execution was not demonstrated in the inspected evidence.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing focused pytest run, a live Postgres migration/helper failure, evidence that quality trend recording can alter gate/workflow authority, or a current artifact hash mismatch in the handoff/outcome-review records would change this to revise.

critical_review.strongest_objection: Claude cited a non-existent pytest node supervisor_quality_trends_parity; this is evidence hygiene noise, not an implementation defect, but it weakens audit traceability of claimed Postgres coverage.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Failing focused pytest, trend recording mutating dual_agent_workflow status or gate events, or supervisor_quality_trends table missing from migration/inline schema equivalence.

assumptions_to_verify: Runtime-native receipts are trustworthy and correspond to the reviewed worktree snapshot.
Production deployments run the Alembic migration chain in the same order as the inline schema.
lesson_task_class is populated on normal workflow routes so trend grouping does not collapse to general.
contradictions_checked: Claude said tests were not run in its review env, but supervisor runtime receipts for outcome_review report passed tests; I treat Claude test_status unknown as reviewer-local, not gate-level.
Prompt tdd.md receipt hash mismatched current file; current handoff, outcome-review, skill-receipts, and local shasum agree, indicating stale packet evidence.
Observed run-end wiring records quality_trends in a try/except and returns gate_authority unchanged, with no call path that advances or blocks gates from metrics.
Status precedence in _payload_accepted checks supervisor_final_status before status and claude_gate_status, and the regression test covers Claude accepted versus supervisor blocked.
Query methods are SELECT-only and tests assert event/row count invariance for query and CLI query.
missing_evidence: Live Postgres migration run with CODEX_SUPERVISOR_POSTGRES_TEST_DSN set.
Live Postgres upsert/query/audit helper exercise for supervisor_quality_trends, not just schema shape.
Fresh local pytest rerun by this reviewer; current decision relies on runtime-native isolated-worktree receipts.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The strongest reason not to advance is that Postgres production behavior is only structurally supported here: table DDL, Alembic migration text, and inline-schema equivalence are present, but live Postgres migration/helper execution was not demonstrated in the inspected evidence.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing focused pytest run, a live Postgres migration/helper failure, evidence that quality trend recording can alter gate/workflow authority, or a current artifact hash mismatch in the handoff/outcome-review records would change this to revise.
